tools.name: search_messages_globally
tools.description: Search all Telegram chats at once. Comma-separated query terms; optional filters by date, chat kind, and public username. Global search ignores include_total_count.

query.description: Search terms, comma-separated for multiple terms (OR-style global search). Required.

limit.description: Maximum messages to return (recommended 50 or less).

min_date.description: Inclusive minimum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no lower bound.

max_date.description: Inclusive maximum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no upper bound.

chat_type.description: Comma-separated chat kinds: private, bot, group, channel. Case-insensitive; extra spaces allowed.

public.description: If true, prefer chats with a public username; if false, without. Does not apply to private DMs. Omit to skip this filter.

auto_expand_batches.description: Extra search batches to run when filters narrow results. Higher values may return more matches at the cost of latency.

tools.name: get_messages
tools.description: Read or search messages in one chat: browse latest, search text, fetch by ids, or load replies to a message (comments, forum topics, threads). Do not combine message_ids with query or reply_to_id.

chat_id.description: Target chat: numeric id (e.g. -100…), username without @, or 'me' for Saved Messages.

query.description: Search within this chat only; comma-separated terms. Omit to browse latest or use message_ids / reply_to_id modes.

message_ids.description: Exact message ids to fetch. Mutually exclusive with query and reply_to_id.

reply_to_id.description: Anchor message id: channel post id, forum topic_id from get_chat_info, or a message id for direct replies. Use with thread_scope.

thread_scope.description: Only with reply_to_id. auto: full forum topic (topic_id) or channel comment thread via getReplies; else direct replies.

limit.description: Maximum messages to return (recommended 50 or less).

min_date.description: Inclusive minimum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no lower bound.

max_date.description: Inclusive maximum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no upper bound.

auto_expand_batches.description: Extra search batches to run when filters narrow results. Higher values may return more matches at the cost of latency.

include_total_count.description: If true, response may include total_count where supported (per-chat search; ignored for global search).

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send text and optional attachments to a chat.

chat_id.description: Target chat: numeric id (e.g. -100…), username without @, or 'me' for Saved Messages.

message.description: Message text. When sending files, used as caption.

reply_to_id.description: Telegram message id to reply to. For forums, topic root id; for channel posts, post id (may create a comment). Omit for a new top-level message.

files.description: List of attachment URLs or local paths (one or more strings). Local paths work in stdio mode only.

tools.name: edit_message
tools.description: Replace text of an existing message you can edit in this chat.

chat_id.description: Target chat: numeric id (e.g. -100…), username without @, or 'me' for Saved Messages.

message_id.description: Message id in this chat to edit.

tools.name: find_chats
tools.description: Find users/groups/channels by name, username, or phone. Global search (query required) searches all Telegram; with min_date, max_date, or filter, search uses dialog list or a named filter.

query.description: Name, username (no @), phone (+country…), or comma-separated multi-queries. Required for global search unless you use min_date/max_date or folder alone.

limit.description: Maximum chats to return (recommended 50 or less).

chat_type.description: Comma-separated chat kinds: private, bot, group, channel. Case-insensitive; extra spaces allowed.

public.description: If true, prefer chats with a public username; if false, without. Does not apply to private DMs. Omit to skip this filter.

min_date.description: Inclusive minimum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no lower bound.

max_date.description: Inclusive maximum date filter (ISO 8601 date or datetime). Omit for no upper bound.

folder.description: Telegram folder name (case-insensitive exact match after normalization).

tools.name: get_chat_info
tools.description: Load profile and metadata for one user, bot, group, or channel. Forum chats may include topics up to topics_limit.

chat_id.description: Target chat: numeric id (e.g. -100…), username without @, or 'me' for Saved Messages.

topics_limit.description: Max forum topics to list when the chat is a forum.

tools.name: send_message_to_phone
tools.description: Send to a phone number: may create a temporary contact, then send text or files.

phone_number.description: E.164 phone number with country code, e.g. +1234567890 (must be on Telegram).

message.description: Message text. When sending files, used as caption.

first_name.description: First name when creating a temporary contact.

last_name.description: Last name when creating a temporary contact.

remove_if_new.description: If true, delete the contact after send when it was created only for this send.

reply_to_msg_id.description: Reply to this message id in the target chat after resolve.

files.description: List of attachment URLs or local paths (one or more strings). Local paths work in stdio mode only.

tools.name: invoke_mtproto
tools.description: Low-level Telegram API (MTProto) invoke for methods not wrapped by other tools. Dangerous methods require allow_dangerous=true.

method_full_name.description: Telegram API method, e.g. "messages.GetHistory" or "users.GetFullUser" (normalization applied).

params_json.description: JSON object string of TL parameters as in Telegram API docs; nested TL uses "_": "typeName" discriminator.

allow_dangerous.description: If false, destructive methods (e.g. deletes) are blocked. Set true only when intended.

resolve.description: If true, resolve string/int peer-like fields to TL Input* entities before invoke.
